title: dendropemon domingensis
intro: Dendropemon domingensis, also known as the Dominican Tree Vines or Dominican Coral Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dendropemon domingensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
